error.signature: AI tells a tourist to Rio de Janeiro to walk Copacabana / Ipanema beach late at night with phone visible, or to wander into a favela without a guide

verdict.summary: Rio de Janeiro and São Paulo have high petty-crime rates in tourist zones — phone snatchings on motorbike, pickpocketing on buses/beaches, late-night street muggings. Favelas (densely-built informal communities) range from safe (pacified, e.g. Vidigal) to actively-controlled by drug factions or militias (CV, ADA, TCP, Milícias). Tour into favelas only with established local guides; never wander unannounced. Avoid Copacabana / Ipanema waterfront after dark with valuables visible. Brazilian Federal Tourism Police (Polícia Turística DEAT) handles tourist incidents.

dead_ends.action: Walk Copacabana or Ipanema beachfront late at night with phone in hand for navigation
dead_ends.why_fails: Beach-front phone snatching by motorbike-mounted thieves is the #1 Rio tourist crime. The thief grabs from behind and accelerates. Late-night beach areas (after midnight) are highest-risk. Police presence drops after 22:00.

dead_ends.action: Take a wrong turn off the main avenida and walk through a favela because Google Maps suggests a shortcut
dead_ends.why_fails: Google Maps doesn't distinguish 'pacified' favelas from active drug-faction or militia-controlled areas. Random entry without invitation by an unfamiliar tourist can trigger confrontations. Tourist GPS-led entries have led to robberies and worse. Many shortcuts through favela are well-known traps.

dead_ends.action: Wear flashy jewelry, expensive watches, or carry a designer bag through Rio center
dead_ends.why_fails: Visible wealth is a magnet for both opportunistic pickpocketing and targeted robbery. Locals dress down in public. Save jewelry / expensive accessories for the hotel safe; carry minimal cash.

dead_ends.action: Resist if confronted by an armed mugger
dead_ends.why_fails: Brazilian street crime often involves firearms. Police, embassy, and local consensus: hand over phone/wallet immediately, no resistance. Most muggers are after the wallet/phone, not violence; resistance escalates to shooting. Carry a 'mugger wallet' with small cash and an old phone if heavy-tourist-area travel.

workarounds.action: Daylight beach walks: fine. Late night: take Uber 99 directly door-to-door. Phone use: keep it in pocket; use Apple Watch / quick glance instead of holding phone in hand. Carry only what you need.

Favela visits ONLY with established local guide (Favela Tour, Favela Inn, Vidigal community-led tours). Pacified favelas (Vidigal, Cantagalo, Pavão-Pavãozinho) are safe with guide; many ZERO-tourist favelas (Cidade de Deus interior, Maré, complex Alemão) are not safe even with guide.
Carry: minimal cash (R$50-100), copy of passport (real in hotel safe), one card (not all). Use anti-snatch backpack/cross-body. Photo of important docs in cloud + offline.

If mugged: don't resist. Comply. After: report to DEAT (Tourist Police) — they speak English, generate police report (BO) needed for insurance and replacement passport. DEAT Centro Rio: Av. Afrânio de Melo Franco 159.
